subject: feat(css): migrate from Pico CSS to Tailwind v4 + DaisyUI v5
body:
Replace Pico CSS v2.1.1 with Tailwind CSS v4.2.2 + DaisyUI v5.5.19 for full
styling control, animations, and customization. Pico's opinionated defaults
caused persistent spacing/wrapping issues and limited visual polish.

- Add Tailwind CSS v4, @tailwindcss/vite plugin, DaisyUI v5
- Create main.css with Tailwind directives, DaisyUI theme config, custom
  animations (scroll-triggered fade-in-up, card hover effects)
- Rewrite index.html with Tailwind utilities + DaisyUI components (navbar,
  card, badge, link), gradient hero text, sticky glassmorphism nav
- Rewrite project.html — meta grid, tabs, doc viewer, markdown renderer
- Update theme.js to set both .dark class (Tailwind) and data-theme (DaisyUI)
- Update flash-prevention scripts for dual class/attribute approach
- Add Intersection Observer for scroll-triggered animations
- Delete pico-theme.css and styles.css (420 lines of Pico overrides)
- Remove @picocss/pico dependency
- Move Tailwind/DaisyUI to devDependencies (build-time only)
- Update README and docs to reflect new tech stack

All accessibility preserved: disclosure pattern burger menu, aria-expanded,
focus-visible outlines, 44px touch targets, safe area insets, print styles.

Tags: css, tailwind, daisyui, migration, ui, animations
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: css-framework-migration
Semver: major
